title: Real-time feeds for market-moving news events?
author: Permtato
selftext:
I'm working on an AI 'assisted' pipeline and wondering if folks might have / be willing to share some ideas. 



For context, I saw a post on one of the trading subs a few months back (buggered if I can find it now though) talking about announcements for 'senior convertible notes offerings' and the typical market reaction when a defined offering window (time period) is given. I did some investigating and found these, and other, announcement types *sometimes* have a pronounced market impact (thought it's not as straight forward as 'offering' = bad). 



Further research showed an abundance of literature around other types of financing announcements and other categories like mergers & acquisitions, earnings, legal, etc and their market impact and it got me thinking about whether there's any way to exploit these events. Unfortunately, a lot of these announcements are made outside of regular trading hours, excluding retail, but some show a continued intraday or +1-3 day move. 

  
So my current pipeline involves automatically scraping announcements from prnewswire, globenewswire, and businesswire. A first-pass LLM call triages all announcements to see if they appear to match any of the categories or subcategories of interest or if they're just noise before a second pass labels them bearish / bullish and provides a 'confidence' level based on basic category-specific prompts. It's all very coarse at the moment and could probably be done with regex alone but after a month or so I intend to pass all that get through the first-pass filter through finer-grained prompts and back-test the signals.



I'm hoping for:

a) general input on / criticisms of the approach. What have I not thought of? what could be better?

b) what categories do you think I should look-out for? I currently have financing, M&A, corporate governance, regulatory / legal / compliance, operational & strategies, and technology & market structure. 

c) sources / news feeds for these types of announcements. 

Initially I'd planned to use SEC EDGAR but rules dictate that, in the case of convertible note offerings for example, the filing can be decoupled from the actual timing of the event (I think the rule is up to 4 days post-event).
